home.title: [MY] Vibe Code Tours — A guided journey into AI-paired coding
home.description: [MY] A cohort-based online tour of the AI coding ecosystem, designed for Myanmar developers ready to build with Claude Code. 8 weeks. 9 students. Free.
home.heroSub: [MY] A cohort-based online tour of the AI coding ecosystem, designed for Myanmar developers ready to build with Claude Code.
home.card1Body: [MY] AI-paired coding fundamentals, prompt craft, team workflows on real projects, and how to ship something useful with Claude as your pair.
home.card2Body: [MY] 2-hour live sessions, twice a week, for 8 weeks. Solo instructor, 9-student cohort, 3 teams of 3. Public Tour Repo. Demo day at the end.
home.card3Body: [MY] Beginners with terminal comfort. Intermediate devs adding AI to their toolkit. Experienced engineers curious about the AI coding landscape, not just one tool.
home.credibility: [MY] Free to participants · Sponsored by [list] · MIT / CC-BY-SA licensed curriculum

about.title: [MY] About — Vibe Code Tours
about.description: [MY] Why Vibe Code Tours exists: a curated entry into the AI coding ecosystem for Myanmar developers, built on awareness over mastery and honesty about what we skip.
about.heading: [MY] Why this Tour exists
about.intro1: [MY] Most coding courses teach one stack. Most AI tutorials teach one tool. Neither helps a developer in 2026, when the landscape changes monthly and the meta-skill is "navigate change."
about.intro2: [MY] We started this Tour for a specific group: Myanmar developers who can follow a terminal, want to build, and need a curated entry into the AI coding ecosystem without paying $1000 for a bootcamp that teaches yesterday's tools.

beliefs.title: [MY] Awareness over mastery.
beliefs.body: [MY] You don't need to master Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and four others. You need to know they exist, what they're for, and how to pick. Mastery comes after.

beliefs.title: [MY] AI is the default partner, not an afterthought.
beliefs.body: [MY] Claude shows up in every chapter — including the team workflows chapter, the deployment chapter, the testing chapter. Not just the "AI module."

beliefs.title: [MY] Projects are vehicles, not outcomes.
beliefs.body: [MY] 60% finished with insight beats 100% finished without. The point is what you understand, not what you shipped.

beliefs.title: [MY] Honest about what we skip.
beliefs.body: [MY] We don't teach a specific framework deeply. We don't certify. We don't place students in jobs. We tell you what we're NOT doing as clearly as what we ARE.

beliefs.title: [MY] Teaching is recursive.
beliefs.body: [MY] Each cohort makes the next cohort better. We publish what we learn. We publish what didn't work.

notList: [MY] A bootcamp with a placement guarantee — there is no placement
[MY] An AI coding tools advertisement — we critique tools openly
[MY] A pure Claude Code class — Claude is the through-line, not the topic
[MY] A beginner-only space — we welcome all levels, with peer-teaching as design
[MY] A Myanmar-exclusive program — applicants from any country welcome, but instruction is in English with Burmese support

team.title: [MY] Team — Vibe Code Tours
team.description: [MY] Who runs Vibe Code Tours: the lead instructor, advisors, and how to help.
team.heading: [MY] Who runs this
team.name: [MY] kokoye2007
team.bio: [MY] Lead instructor of Vibe Code Tours. I build with AI coding tools and run this cohort to help Myanmar developers find their footing in the fast-moving AI coding landscape — awareness over mastery, AI as the default pair. Most of my work lives on GitHub.
team.advisorsBody: [MY] (Optional — list mentors, technical advisors, past students serving as TAs.)
team.helpBody: [MY] We accept contributions of feedback, sponsorship, and time. See the sponsors page for how to contribute.

curriculum.title: [MY] Curriculum — Vibe Code Tours
curriculum.description: [MY] 8 chapters over 8 weeks. AI-paired coding from setup to demo day, structured by the A/B/C tier framework.
curriculum.heading: [MY] What you'll learn
curriculum.intro: [MY] 8 chapters over 8 weeks. 2 sessions × 2 hours per chapter. Plus async pre-class setup (Chapter 0). Cohort runs 16 live sessions across the 8-week arc.
curriculum.tierIntro: [MY] Every chapter is built from blocks tagged by tier. The mix shows how AI fits into that chapter:
curriculum.tierA: [MY] Tier A — AI off. Manual demo, bootstrap, human ceremony.
curriculum.tierB: [MY] Tier B — Foundation. You do it manually first, then with AI.
curriculum.tierC: [MY] Tier C — AI default. Claude does the work, you validate.
curriculum.tierNote: [MY] We deliberately balance the ratio per chapter. Ch0 is all A (manual setup). Ch2 is heavy B+C (learning Claude). Ch8 is back to A (demos are human).
get: [MY] A public PR on the Tour Repo (portfolio)
commit: [MY] Show up to 2/3 of sessions minimum
[MY] One honest retrospective at the end

faq.title: [MY] FAQ — Vibe Code Tours
faq.description: [MY] Frequently asked questions about Vibe Code Tours: is it free, certificates, time commitment, joining from abroad, and more.
faq.heading: [MY] Frequently asked questions

items.a: [MY] Yes. We're sponsor-funded. We don't take money from students. We may ask for an optional voluntary contribution at the end if you found it valuable, but it's never required.
[MY] We tour the AI coding ecosystem — multiple tools, multiple workflows, multiple model providers. Not a bootcamp on one stack.

[MY] Finish the Tour and find out. We don't lead with a certificate — a PDF isn't why you came. But complete the journey and there's a hidden gem waiting at the end for those who make it through. Along the way you also earn the things that actually carry weight: a public PR on the Tour Repo (your portfolio), a demo day recording (your work, shareable), and a reference letter on request. The gem is the reward; the work is the proof.

items.q: [MY] Can I join from outside Myanmar?
items.a: [MY] Yes. Instruction is English. Cohort schedule is Myanmar evening / SE Asia evening, which works for ~half the planet.

items.q: [MY] How much time do I need per week?
items.a: [MY] 5-8 hours outside the 4 hours of class. Honest answer: closer to 8 if you're newer.

items.q: [MY] What if I can't make a session?
items.a: [MY] Up to one-third of sessions can be missed without penalty (recordings provided). Beyond that you risk losing your cohort spot.

items.q: [MY] What if I don't have a project idea?
items.a: [MY] That's normal for beginners. Chapter 3 helps you find one. Many students enter with no idea and leave with a working prototype.

items.q: [MY] Will you teach a specific tool?
items.a: [MY] We touch on Claude Code (deeply), plus Cursor, Copilot, Aider, and the broader landscape. We don't promise depth on any single tool besides Claude Code.

items.q: [MY] What if I don't speak English well?
items.a: [MY] We provide Burmese support in channel for questions. Course materials are English; we recommend Claude itself for translation help — meta, but it works.
[MY] No. We teach AI-paired CODING. Claude is the AI; you're the coder. We don't teach how to build models — we teach how to ship with them.
[MY] If Cohort 1 goes well. Subscribe via the apply page.
[MY] See the sponsors page for organization-level support or individual donations. Or share this site with one developer who'd benefit.

items.q: [MY] I have a question not answered here.
